title: Marisol Cove boutique-hotel homepage hero: license widescreen stock, crop desktop + mobile banners, grade to warm coastal look, place vectorized logo, InDesign export to web JPEG/PNG + print PDF
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: the client wants to "create premium, luxury-style website banners" and specifically "Homepage hero banner designs for both: Desktop [and] Mobile" with a "Clean, premium, modern design" and the "Ability to maintain consistent brand identity across all creative assets," and explicitly asks to "Preserve our exact logo ... and branding." REGROUNDED to the hospitality_travel vertical (the slot's target) and made IP-clean: the real Shopify-apparel client is replaced by a fictional boutique hotel, "Marisol Cove," and the deliverable stays a desktop + mobile homepage hero banner pair with a preserved logo. IMPORTANT — the original brief's "bonus" ask ("AI image generation and editing," creating "realistic AI fashion models," and "Expand our range of model imagery") is DELIBERATELY DROPPED because it is out of connector scope and banned here; per the slot replacement_note this O8 stock-hero task instead licenses a correctly-sized widescreen coastal stock photo, crops/reframes it into the desktop and mobile hero frames, grades it to a warm coastal look, and places the client's preserved logo. NO generative expand / outpaint / upscale — a large-enough stock hero is licensed up front and cropped down, never a small image stretched up.
one_line_ask: License a large widescreen coastal stock photo, crop it into desktop and mobile homepage hero frames, grade both to a warm coastal look, place the client's vectorized Marisol Cove logo, and export web-ready JPEG/PNG banners plus a print-ready PDF proof from the client's authored InDesign banner template.
full_brief: Marisol Cove is a fictional boutique clifftop hotel on the Amalfi-style coast, refreshing its homepage. We need a premium, luxury-style homepage hero banner in two sizes — a Desktop hero (1920x720 px, wide cinematic band) and a Mobile hero (1080x1350 px, tall portrait) — that share one consistent image and brand identity. We do NOT have our own hero photography yet, so please license a suitable high-resolution widescreen coastal shot from Adobe Stock (think: turquoise sea, terraced clifftop villas, warm late-afternoon light, generous empty sky on one side so our headline and logo have room to breathe) — it must be large enough to crop both the wide desktop band and the tall mobile frame without any upscaling. Grade the licensed image to our warm "golden-hour coastal" look: lift the exposure a touch, warm the white balance, push vibrance so the sea and terracotta pop, and keep it clean and premium (no heavy filters). For both crops leave clear open space on the left third for our headline "Escape to the Cove" and our logo lockup — do not let the buildings or horizon crowd that zone. We are handing you our exact wordmark as a flat PNG (cream "Marisol Cove" with a small wave mark) — please preserve it exactly, vectorize it to a crisp SVG, and place it top-left in both banners; do not redraw or restyle it. We are also handing you our authored InDesign banner template (marisol_hero_template.indd) that already contains the desktop and mobile artboards with a background image frame, the logo frame, and a <<headline>> / <<tagline>> data field pair, plus a one-row CSV with our copy — please run the merge so the copy and placed assets bind, then export: a web JPEG of the desktop hero, a web PNG of the mobile hero, and a print-ready PDF proof of both artboards for our sign-off deck. Brand colors are deep teal (#0E5A62), terracotta (#C4652E) and cream (#F4EBDD).

inputs.name: marisol_hero_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) containing two artboards for the Marisol Cove homepage hero — a Desktop artboard sized 1920x720 px and a Mobile artboard sized 1080x1350 px. Each artboard has a full-bleed background image frame (for the graded coastal crop), a logo frame anchored top-left, and a text area bound to REAL InDesign data-merge fields named <<headline>> and <<tagline>>. Left third of each artboard kept clear of the background focal subject for the headline/logo lockup. Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own authored banner template — a normal freelancer hand-off. document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields; it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input, not a chain output. Two artboards in one .indd is the realistic way a designer ships a desktop+mobile pair.

inputs.name: marisol_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat boutique-hotel wordmark logo reading 'MARISOL COVE' in an elegant high-contrast serif, cream (#F4EBDD) letters with a small deep-teal (#0E5A62) minimalist wave glyph above the text, on a fully transparent background. Clean flat raster PNG, crisp edges, high contrast, no gradients or drop shadows, suitable for vectorizing to SVG.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image renders clean legible logo text; flat high-contrast transparent PNG so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. Supplied as the 'exact logo' the brief says must be preserved — never redrawn, only vectorized and placed.

inputs.name: marisol_hero_copy.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a one-row CSV with columns headline,tagline holding the hero copy: headline 'Escape to the Cove' and tagline 'A clifftop retreat above the Amalfi blue.' Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<headline>> and <<tagline>>.
inputs.realism_notes: One-row copy CSV; headers match the authored .indd merge fields so the [T] data-merge binds the headline/tagline into both artboards.

outputs.name: Marisol_Hero_desktop.jpg
outputs.spec: Web-ready 1920x720 px JPEG of the desktop homepage hero: warm golden-hour coastal crop, headline/tagline bound from CSV, vectorized Marisol Cove logo placed top-left, left-third clear for copy. Max quality, sRGB.

outputs.name: Marisol_Hero_mobile.png
outputs.spec: Web-ready 1080x1350 px PNG of the mobile homepage hero: same graded image reframed to the tall portrait crop, headline/tagline bound, logo placed top-left.

outputs.name: Marisol_Hero_proof.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready PDF proof containing both artboards (desktop + mobile) exported from the merged InDesign document for the founder sign-off deck; ~300 DPI.

outputs.name: graded_hero_crops_x2
outputs.spec: The licensed coastal stock image, warm-graded and cropped into the two hero frames: a 1920x720 desktop band and a 1080x1350 mobile portrait, both with the focal subject kept off the left third.

outputs.name: marisol_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the preserved Marisol Cove wordmark for crisp scaling across both banner sizes.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a high-resolution widescreen coastal shot (turquoise sea, terraced clifftop villas, warm light, open sky on one side). Returns low-res candidates to choose from.
License and download the chosen coastal hero at full resolution (license BEFORE use). Must be large enough to crop both the wide desktop and tall mobile frames with no upscaling.
Auto-tone the licensed stock to normalize exposure/contrast before the warm grade (applied once on the shared master so both crops match).
Lift the exposure a touch for the bright, airy premium coastal feel the brief asks for.
Warm the white balance toward the golden-hour coastal look (sun-warm terracotta and sand).
Push vibrance so the turquoise sea and terracotta villas pop while staying clean/premium. This is the shared graded master both crops derive from.
Reframe the graded master to the 1920x720 desktop band, aligning the focal villas to the right so the left third stays clear for the headline/logo (align x~0.67).
Reframe the SAME graded master to the 1080x1350 tall mobile frame, again keeping the subject off the left third — same source so both banners are visually consistent.
Vectorize the preserved flat wordmark PNG into a crisp SVG so the logo stays razor-sharp at both banner sizes. Logo is preserved, not redrawn.
Create a Creative Cloud project folder (Marisol Cove / homepage-hero) to hold the prepped assets for the merge.
Initialize an upload session for the prepped banner assets (two graded crops + vector logo) into the project folder.
Finalize the upload; returns the asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the background and logo frames.

InDesign data-merge: bind the headline/tagline row into the client's authored .indd (real <<headline>>/<<tagline>> fields); the desktop/mobile graded crops sit in the background frames and the vector logo in the logo frames. [T] requires the user-authored template.
Export the desktop artboard of the merged document to a max-quality web JPEG deliverable (1920x720).
Export the mobile artboard of the merged document to a web PNG deliverable (1080x1350).
Export both artboards of the merged document to a print-ready ~300 DPI PDF proof for the founder sign-off deck.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining throughout. The stock hero chains asset_search(1) -> license(2). That single licensed image runs a 4-link grade pipeline as a shared master: coastal_hero_fullres -> auto_tone(3) -> exposure(4) -> color_temperature(5) -> vibrance(6) = hero_graded_master, each step consuming only the prior step's output. The master then forks into two crops — desktop(7) and mobile(8) — both consuming hero_graded_master so the two banners share one graded source (the brief's 'consistent brand identity across all creative assets'). The preserved wordmark PNG chains to vectorize(9). asset_create_folders(10) makes the project folder that feeds the upload; the two crops + vector logo + folder converge into upload_session(11) -> finalize(12) -> data-merge(13), which also consumes the authored .indd and the copy CSV. The merged .indd(13) is the single source for all three exports — desktop JPEG(14), mobile PNG(15), proof PDF(16). Only step 1 (search), the three supplied inputs (template, wordmark PNG, copy CSV), and the folder-create seed originate outside the chain; the final banners emerge only from the assembled chain — no image is ever upscaled or generatively expanded.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon: 16 connector calls across 3 tool groups (image_* raster grading/crop/vectorize, asset_* stock+storage, document_* InDesign merge/export), with 13 distinct Adobe tools — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from sourcing and licensing a correctly-sized stock hero, running a 4-step warm grade on a shared master, forking it into two aspect-distinct hero crops (wide desktop band + tall mobile portrait) that must stay visually consistent and keep the left third clear, vectorizing and preserving the client logo, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge with a triple export (JPEG/PNG/PDF). It respects every hard limit: this is the GEN-FREE O8 stock-hero pattern — a large stock image is licensed then cropped DOWN, never a small image expanded up; NO image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / generative fill / text-to-image / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the InDesign layout is authored by the client (supplied .indd input), and document_render_layout / document_merge_data_layout only bind and EXPORT it, not compose it.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used: no image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop (the hero is licensed at full-res then cropped DOWN into both frames — never a small image stretched up); no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo (the wordmark is a SUPPLIED PNG, only vectorized and placed, never drawn); no upscaling / super-resolution (both crops are extracted from a larger licensed master); no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account (all steps server=='adobe').
reverify.notes: Potentially-risky steps stay in-bounds: (1) image_crop_and_resize(7,8) forks ONE licensed full-res master into two smaller aspect-distinct crops (reframe/extract = downscale+reframe, not upscale) — the license step ensures the source is large enough and the crops keep the subject off the left third via align, no canvas is added. (2) asset_license_and_download_stock(2) licenses BEFORE use, correct Stock workflow. (3) document_merge_data_layout(13) is correctly tagged [T]; the authored marisol_hero_template.indd carrying real <<headline>>/<<tagline>> fields is a SUPPLIED input (kind:data, gen_model 'n/a-authored-asset'), and the CSV headers match the merge fields, so it genuinely binds — the connector does not compose the layout. (4) document_render_layout(14,15,16) only EXPORTS the merged authored .indd to JPEG/PNG/PDF, composing nothing. (5) image_vectorize(9) converts a supplied flat PNG logo to SVG (in-palette). No Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design), no Canva tools, no [L] local step. Grounded in a real Upwork 'Shopify banner design' brief, regrounded IP-clean to a fictional boutique hotel; the brief's out-of-scope AI-model-generation bonus was deliberately dropped per the slot's gen-free replacement_note.
